title: QA followups (S90/S96 verification) + entity create/delete probes + input inject + game-design dogfood

stories.title: POST /__agf/entity creates a new entity with an initial component map
stories.summary: Write counterpart to S097 AGF-PROBE-ENTITY-DUMP. Body: `{ entityId: string, components: Record<string, unknown> }`. Server-side calls world.addEntity(entityId) + world.setComponent for each entry. Refuses to clobber an existing id with 409 AGF_PROBE_ENTITY_EXISTS. Returns the same shape as the GET path so the caller can confirm. Useful for tests + for game-design agent dogfooding (drop a fixture entity to verify a system).
verification: acceptance: POST /__agf/entity with { entityId: 'qa-probe-1', components: { Foo: { x: 1 } } } creates the entity; a subsequent GET /__agf/entity/qa-probe-1 returns the same components. Re-posting the same id returns 409 AGF_PROBE_ENTITY_EXISTS.
engine/runtime/start.ts: createEntity helper returns { kind: 'ok' | 'entity-exists' }
engine/dev/agf-dev-bridge.ts: POST /entity validates body shape (entityId string + components object); reuses parseEntityPath where it can
+ unit tests on body validator: missing entityId, missing components, components non-object, entity-exists conflict

stories.title: DELETE /__agf/entity/<entityId> removes an entity wholesale
stories.summary: Completes the entity CRUD triplet (create / read / delete + S097's component write). Server-side calls world.removeEntity(entityId). Reuses parseEntityPath. Returns 404 AGF_PROBE_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND when the entity is unknown. Useful when an agent's automation script needs to clean up after itself, or when running probes against an isolated test entity that should not leak between runs.
verification: acceptance: After POST /__agf/entity { entityId: 'test.1', components: {} }, DELETE /__agf/entity/test.1 returns 200 with { deleted: 'test.1' }; a subsequent GET /__agf/entity/test.1 returns 404 AGF_PROBE_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND.
engine/runtime/start.ts: deleteEntity(entityId) returns { kind: 'ok' | 'entity-not-found' }
engine/dev/agf-dev-bridge.ts: DELETE /entity/:entityId reuses parseEntityPath
+ unit test on the entity-not-found path

stories.title: POST /__agf/input/action lets QA fire game inputs without DOM focus (QA-006)
stories.summary: Promoted from QA-2026-05-20-006. Today's player-input-system listens on window keydown events, and browsers refuse to dispatch trusted KeyboardEvents from page.evaluate / Playwright keyboard.press once the HUD overlay grabs focus. QA can't verify visual gameplay features (bomb-wiggle, death-fall, danger overlay) because they can't fire the bomb-place action from automation. Add a project-agnostic POST /__agf/input/action that takes `{ entityId, action, value? }` and writes a generic `InputAction` transient onto the entity. Project-local input systems can consume that component the same way they consume keyboard events. Reuses the engine's existing `applyCommands` machinery on the runtime; no new auth surface. Pairs with the planned game-design dogfood (proposals written this sprint will be verified next sprint using this probe).
verification: acceptance: POST /__agf/input/action { entityId: 'player.1', action: 'place-bomb' } from a Playwright session triggers the same downstream effect as pressing Space in the page — a Bomb entity spawns on the player's cell on the next fixedUpdate. Idempotent within a tick (multiple POSTs in the same frame still produce one bomb due to the BomberStats cap).
engine/runtime/start.ts: runtime.injectInput(entityId, action, value?) writes an InputAction transient; the runtime returns { kind: 'ok' | 'entity-not-found' }.
engine/dev/agf-dev-bridge.ts: POST /input/action validates the body shape; missing entityId / action returns 400 AGF_BRIDGE_INVALID_INPUT_ACTION.
examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/player-input-system.ts grows an InputAction reader path so 'place-bomb' / 'move-up' / 'move-down' / 'move-left' / 'move-right' / 'restart' all work via the probe.
+ unit tests on the body validator + on the Kaboom Crew adoption (place-bomb via InputAction component spawns a bomb)

stories.title: engine-cli 'prints a stable inspect summary' test flakes at 5000ms (QA-004)
stories.summary: Promoted from QA-2026-05-20-004. The integration test in tests/unit/engine-cli.test.ts that spawns `tsx engine/tools/cli.ts inspect ...` occasionally exceeds the 5000ms vitest default timeout. ~1/3 of runs fail in QA's environment. The root cause is the cold-start cost of tsx + the full CLI dependency graph; the actual inspect logic is cheap. Fix: bump the test's timeout to 15000ms AND pre-warm the tsx process via a `beforeAll` hook so subsequent runs hit a warm cache (when the test is part of a larger run). Keep the test's actual assertions unchanged.
verification: acceptance: Running `npx vitest run tests/unit/engine-cli.test.ts` 5 times in a row never reports a timeout in the 'prints a stable inspect summary' case.
tests/unit/engine-cli.test.ts: the affected `it()` gets a per-test timeout override + a one-time tsx warmup
Sprint summary acknowledges this is a test-infra fix, not a product change

stories.title: Lock the engine-cli flake fix with a deterministic timing test (QA-005)
stories.summary: Promoted from QA-2026-05-20-005 (regressionFor QA-004). After BUG-ENGINE-CLI-INSPECT-SUMMARY-FLAKE lands, add a regression test that runs the inspect summary 3 times back-to-back and asserts each completes in <10 s. Catches future regressions where someone removes the warmup or bloats the dependency graph again.
verification: acceptance: A new test runs inspect 3x in a row + asserts each finishes; would fail on the original 5000ms-timeout codebase.
tests/unit/engine-cli.test.ts: new 'engine-cli inspect: 3x warm runs all finish < 10 s' case

stories.title: First end-to-end dogfood of the propose:new → propose:promote flow
stories.summary: Exercise the S097-shipped game-design agent flow with real proposals. (1) `npm run propose:new -- "Decal-on-grid renderer primitive (M27)" --kind feature --priority should --epic M27-DECAL-LAYER` + fill in intent/rationale/acceptanceHints from notes/dynabomber-readiness-analysis.md §14. (2) `npm run propose:new -- "Region-rule modifier primitive (M28)" --kind mechanic --priority could --epic M28-REGION-RULES` + fill in. (3) Verify both pass `npm run backlog:check`. (4) Create a pending S099 sprint shell so `npm run propose:promote -- --into S099 --skip-check` has a target. (5) Promote both proposals. (6) Verify the S099 sprint JSON now has two pending stories with FEAT- prefixes and the source files moved under archive/S099/.
verification: acceptance: At the end of this story, S099.sprint.json (pending) exists with at least 2 pending FEAT-* stories that came from the two GDP proposals; backlog/proposed-stories/archive/S099/ contains the source files; backlog/proposed-stories/ (current) is empty again.
Captures any gaps in the S097 tooling that the dogfood reveals (e.g. unclear error messages) as inline comments in the sprint summary so a follow-up story can address them.

stories.title: docs/qa/invalid-ticket-handling.md — how dev rejects QA tickets that don't apply
stories.summary: Define the convention captured ad-hoc in this sprint (rejecting QA-001 + QA-003). When dev decides a QA ticket should NOT promote into a sprint (already-fixed, intentional behavior, duplicate, etc.), they `git mv` the JSON file under `backlog/qa-tickets/archive/rejected/` and append a one-line rationale to that directory's README.md. QA can still see the ticket; the directory tells them dev considered it and declined. Documents both halves: when dev rejects (and why), and what QA does when they see a ticket get rejected (don't refile; argue in a comment if needed).
verification: acceptance: docs/qa/invalid-ticket-handling.md exists and answers: 'when does dev reject a ticket?', 'where does it land?', 'what does QA do next?'. Cross-links to docs/qa/design.md and docs/qa/agent.md.
The README.md inside backlog/qa-tickets/archive/rejected/ (already created this sprint) gets a link to the new doc.

stories.title: Danger overlay on cells inside a bomb's projected blast radius
stories.summary: Visual readability polish: while a bomb's fuse is < 1 s remaining, paint a translucent red overlay on every cell that the eventual blast will cover. Distinct from the post-detonation BlastTile (which already exists) — this is the PREDICTION layer so the player can dodge in time. Uses the existing `projectedBlastCells(world, gx, gz, range)` helper from examples/kaboom-crew/src/danger.ts. Tile component: project-local DangerTile entity (color: '#ff3a3a', alpha: 0.35, layer: 'fx-below') auto-removed when the bomb detonates or its fuse > 1 s again. Lays groundwork for the M27 decal layer epic without requiring it.
verification: acceptance: As any bomb's fuse drops below 1 s, every cell it will eventually scorch lights up red until detonation; the red disappears cleanly when the blast resolves.
examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/danger-decal-system.ts: walks Bomb entities with fuseRemaining < 1 s; reuses projectedBlastCells; spawns/removes per-cell DangerTile entities
+ unit tests: a bomb with fuseRemaining=0.5 spawns DangerTiles for every projected cell; fuseRemaining=2 spawns none; removing the bomb removes its DangerTiles
